message:
test(readme): cover Series.shift/diff/pct_change/rank (fd90.249 / br-tblla)

Extends readme_element_wise_operations_compiles_and_runs to cover the
4 Series sequential/ranking ops at fp-frame:4206-6461 that previously
had no integration coverage:

- Series.shift(periods)
- Series.diff(periods)
- Series.pct_change(periods)
- Series.rank(method, ascending, na_option)

DataFrame counterparts were already exercised earlier in the test.

Verified: 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -812,6 +812,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenpandas-t7no","title":"CONFORMANCE: pandas merge/join/concat parity matrix","description":"Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses: add 5-10 live pandas oracle parity cases for merge/join/concat across empty inputs, single-row inputs, NaN keys, duplicate keys, mixed dtypes, outer/inner ordering, and index alignment edge cases. Land any divergence as [HIGH] review bead with failing input + actual + expected.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T03:23:00.355993676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T06:40:52.938804717Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T06:40:52.936935097Z","close_reason":"Added 10 live-oracle merge/join/concat parity matrix cases","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","join","parity-matrix"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-t7wn","title":"pandas/io/sql: DataFrame.to_sql(index=..., index_label=...) write-index parity","description":"py signature:
DataFrame.to_sql("results", con, index=True, index_label="row_id", if_exists="fail") -> None

Current fp-io write_sql only writes data columns and always drops the DataFrame index. Add pandas-style index/index_label support so the index can be emitted as one or more SQL columns with the requested label.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T12:07:29.680444155Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T12:58:46.649897570Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T12:58:46.647949310Z","close_reason":"Implemented SQL write index parity in commit f13e1f4","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-t8z","title":"Add DataFrame.at_time conformance test coverage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-17T08:07:55.412306865Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-17T08:10:47.848434455Z","closed_at":"2026-04-17T08:10:47.845910288Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-tblla","title":"fd90.249: Cover Series.diff/shift/pct_change/rank","description":"fd90.249: Cover Series.diff / shift / pct_change / rank.

Series sequential / ranking ops with no integration coverage:
- Series.shift(periods) (line 4206)
- Series.diff(periods) (line 4237)
- Series.pct_change(periods) (line 5433)
- Series.rank(method, ascending, na_option) (line 6461)

DataFrame counterparts are tested.

Scope:
- Extend an existing test with all 4 calls.

DataFrame counterparts were already tested. All 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-tc3g2","title":"fd90.193: Integration test for README Reshaping section","description":"fd90.193: Add integration test for README "Reshaping" section (lines 498-524).

The Reshaping section documents 7 reshaping APIs; only pivot_table has coverage. Missing: melt, stack, unstack, crosstab, crosstab_normalize, get_dummies, xs.

Scope (one new test):
- DataFrame.melt(id_vars, value_vars, var_name, value_name) — wide → long
- DataFrame.stack — column index → row index
- DataFrame.unstack — row index → column index
- DataFrame.crosstab(&gender, &dept) — contingency table
- DataFrame.crosstab_normalize(&gender, &dept, "all") — with normalization
- DataFrame.get_dummies(&["col1", "col2"]) — one-hot encoding
- DataFrame.xs(&IndexLabel) — cross-section selection

Files: crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs (single new test, ~50 lines).
Time estimate: 30-40min.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-26T19:44:15.965710531Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T19:46:16.753514886Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T19:46:16.751457721Z","close_reason":"Added readme_reshaping_compiles_and_runs covering 7 reshaping APIs: melt (wide→long with id_vars/value_vars and custom var/value names), stack/unstack (column ↔ row index round-trip), crosstab (contingency table from two categorical Series), crosstab_normalize ('all' mode), get_dummies (one-hot on multiple columns), xs (cross-section using From<&str> for IndexLabel). All 32 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-tem9","title":"fd90.79: fix DISC-012 - parse_datetime_string_with_timezone_pattern coerces outliers to NaT","description":"Implement the fix for DISC-012 (refined by fd90.78). parse_datetime_string_with_timezone_pattern currently coerces opposite-pattern rows to NaT, causing parse_csv_datetime_column to bail on mixed-tz CSV columns and leave them as raw input strings. Fix: in the Naive branch, when has_tz_suffix is true, fall through to parse_tz_aware_datetime + format_aware_datetime instead of returning NaT. Same idea (with naive parser fallback) for the Aware branch when has_tz_suffix is false. The infer_mixed_timezone flag's whole point is to support mixed columns, so individual outliers should parse independently while the inferred pattern controls the column-level dtype intent. Test: FP-P2D-429 packet should go from FAIL to PASS after the fix; existing parse_dates tests still green.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-26T07:51:21.871924414Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T07:56:00.015040659Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T07:56:00.012408275Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","fd90","fix","review"]}
